---
title: "Andy Shen"
image: andy.jpg
subtitle: "Statistician at Genentech"
about:
  id: hero-heading
  template: trestles
  image-shape: rectangle
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: aashen@berkeley.edu
      href: mailto:aashen@berkeley.edu
    - icon: github
      text: aashen12
      href: https://github.com/aashen12
    - text: "{{< ai google-scholar >}} Google Scholar"
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=5GYeqKgAAAAJ&hl=en
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/shen-andy/
---

::: {#hero-heading}
**As of April 2026, I am a Statistician at Genentech in the Product Development Data Sciences group.**

I completed my PhD in Statistics at UC Berkeley in 2026, where I was advised by [Haiyan Huang](https://statistics.berkeley.edu/people/haiyan-huang), [Sam Pimentel](https://statistics.berkeley.edu/people/sam-pimentel), and [Avi Feller](https://statistics.berkeley.edu/people/avi-feller). I received my B.S. in Statistics from UCLA in 2021.

My research was motivated by causal inference problems in health policy and medicine, with a focus on developing methods for robust and transparent causal inference. I continue to be actively engaged in research at the intersection of causal inference, machine learning, and survival analysis. My doctoral work was supported by the NSF Graduate Research Fellowship.
:::

# Recent News

-   **April 2026:** I have completed my PhD in Statistics at UC Berkeley and joined Genentech as a Statistician.

-   **December 2025:** Our paper on using tree-ensembles to estimate better weights for causal inference is now on [arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.11751)!

-   **May 2025:** I started my internship at Genentech in the Product Development Data Sciences group.

-   **May 2025:** Our [paper](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2001037025001667?via%3Dihub) on analyzing multiple sclerosis progression with volumetric MRI has been accepted in *Computational and Structural Biotechnology Journal!*

-   **February 2025:** Our paper on sensitivity analysis for causal decompositions is [published](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/sim.70010) in *Statistics in Medicine!*

-   **January 2025:** Our paper on sensitivity analysis for causal decompositions received student paper awards for the 2025 International Conference on Health Policy Statistics and the [2025 Joint Statistical Meetings](https://community.amstat.org/mhs/awards/studentpapercompetition).

```{=html}
<script src="https://cdn.counter.dev/script.js" data-id="fdcc1b2d-f51b-4eee-9400-5bac1da14f97" data-utcoffset="-7"></script>
```
